taxonomy: Cynanchica lutea is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family. This plant is commonly known as the yellow trumpetvine. It is native to the southeastern United States and is often found growing in woodland edges and along streams. The classification of Cynanchica lutea places it within the tribe Asclepiadeae, which is a group of plants known for their unique flowers and seed pods. The plant is also sometimes referred to as the yellow swallow-wort.
